name: PERSISTENT FETAL VASCULATURE
definition: PERSISTENT HYPERPLASTIC PRIMARY VITREUS rare congenital nonhereditary condition with persistence + hyperplasia of fibrovascular important source of intraocular nutrition until 8th month of gestation; arises from dorsal fibrillar ectodermal meshwork + mesodermal tissue consisting of embryonic hyaloid

demographics.Age: noticed at birth / within first few weeks of life

dominant_finding: linear band / septum extending to posterior pole
discriminating_features: enhancing cone-shaped central retrolental density extending from lens through vitreous

US: hyperechoic band extending from posterior pole of globe to posterior surface of lens (=
central anechoic line (= persistent hyaloid artery) with positive Doppler signals
hyperechoic band extending from papilla to ora serrata (= retinal detachment)
heterogeneous increased echogenicity of vitreus ← hemorrhage
CT: enhancing cone-shaped central retrolental density extending from lens through vitreous
linear band / septum extending to posterior pole
abnormally small lucent rounded lens ← absorption / swelling
fluid-fluid levels from breakdown of recurrent hemorrhage in subhyaloid (between vitreus
MR: hyperintense vitreous body on T1WI + T2WI from chronic blood degradation products
hypo- to isointense thin triangular band with base near optic disc and apex at posterior
marked enhancement of fibrovascular mass within vitreus
